tweetText: A new project called ReasoningBank helps AI agents learn from their experiences, enhancing their problem-solving skills. This could lead to more intelligent and adaptable AI systems in the future. via Google Research Blog.

Snowflake is expanding its AI platforms, introducing Snowflake Intelligence for business users and Cortex Code for developers. This move aims to enhance AI deployment and development within its ecosystem. via AI News.

Siemens has launched the Eigen Engineering Agent, an AI that autonomously plans and validates automation engineering tasks. This innovation streamlines workflows, enhancing efficiency in engineering processes. via AI News.
Meta is tracking employees' keystrokes and mouse movements to gather data for AI training. This raises concerns about privacy and surveillance in the workplace. via TechCrunch AI
An unauthorized group reportedly accessed Anthropic's exclusive cyber tool, Mythos. This raises concerns about cybersecurity and the potential misuse of AI tools. via TechCrunch AI

SpaceX is collaborating with Cursor and has the option to acquire the startup for $60B. This partnership highlights the challenges both companies face in competing with top AI firms like Anthropic and OpenAI. via TechCrunch AI
AI lab NeoCognition raised $40M to create agents that learn like humans and can master any field. This funding could revolutionize how AI adapts and grows in knowledge. via TechCrunch AI
OpenAI's ChatGPT Images 2.0 model is impressively skilled at generating text alongside images. This leap in AI capability highlights the rapid advancements in technology. via TechCrunch AI

Sam Altman criticized Anthropic's new cybersecurity model, Mythos, for using "fear-based marketing" to boost its appeal. This highlights ongoing competition in AI and raises questions about ethical marketing practices. via TechCrunch AI

Clarifai has deleted 3 million photos from OkCupid used to train its facial recognition AI following an FTC settlement. This action highlights the ongoing scrutiny of data privacy in AI development. via TechCrunch AI
